methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
Space Forge is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $22.6M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 private peers in the same sector at Series A or Series B stages (ClearSpace, Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network), with valuations ranging from $400M to $2.0B. Five public-market comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) exist in the sector but cannot be applied given the absence of Space Forge's revenue data.

Two quantitative methods triangulate to a valuation range. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $23M raised proxy, implies $45M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median of $850M, with a floor of $400M and ceiling of $2.0B. These methods reflect fundamentally different assumptions: stage-progression anchors to capital deployed, while peer-median weights comparable transaction multiples.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $38M to $977M, with a median of $448M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Space Forge's risk and development trajectory better align with early-stage capital metrics or with late-stage peer valuations. Key uncertainties include revenue traction (unavailable), customer contracts, and development milestones relative to the peer set. Public comparables could narrow this range but require revenue disclosure.
